perturbation.original_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 10 year( s) from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 5 year( s) from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by modifying the automatic termination date of the agreement. Initially, it was set to 10 years from the Effective Date, but now it's changed to 5 years. This directly conflicts with the later part of the same section, and creates uncertainty about the actual duration of the agreement. The contradiction affects enforcement by creating uncertainty as to the agreements validity
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 5 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the automatic termination date of the agreement from 10 years to 5 years. The contradicted text repeats that the agreement shall automatically expire and terminate 5 years from the Effective Date, yet also states that the agreement may be terminated at any time with 15 days prior written notice. The change introducing a '5 year' term clashes with the intial '10 year' term, making it a strong contradiction that introduces uncertainty in the contract as stated in the Perturbation Explanation and follows the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction type.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement will continue in full force and effect until so terminated.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement will continue in full force and effect for 2 years after termination.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 5. Originally, the agreement continued until terminated. Now, it continues for 2 years *after* termination. This contradicts the original concept of termination ending the agreement and creates confusion about the actual duration and ongoing obligations, creating an uncertainty about the actual duration of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 5 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect for 2 years after termination. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement continues until terminated. The changed text states it continues for 2 years *after* termination. The contradicted text contains the statement 'This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 5 year( s) from the Effective Date' and also 'The Agreement will continue in full force and effect for 2 years after termination'. Since the text was added to the contradicted text, it creates an in-text contradiction. The first sentence says that the agreement terminates after 5 years, while the addition states that the agreement continues for 2 years after termination, directly contradicting the termination clause within the same section. This is an inconsistency as defined above.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within five (5) business days after the date of such destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within thirty (30) business days after the date of such destruction.
perturbation.explanation: This modification extends the period for returning or destroying confidential information from five to thirty business days after termination or request. This contradicts the implicit urgency suggested in other sections regarding protecting confidential information and could lead to disputes over timely compliance. It creates an in-text contradiction within the section itself, creating uncertainty regarding the return date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 5 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect for 2 years after termination. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within thirty (30) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for returning or destroying confidential information from five to thirty business days. The contradicted text includes the statement that "The confidentiality obligations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r." While confidentiality survives for a year, the extended timeframe of 30 days for returning or destroying information introduces a conflict and weakens the confidentiality requirements, creating uncertainty about timely compliance and contradicts the implicitly urgent nature of protecting confidential information that is also in the contradited text. This creates a strong inconsistency and thus, an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of one (1) yea r.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of two (2) yea rs, unless stated otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the survival period of confidentiality obligations post-termination from one year to two years and adds 'unless stated otherwise'. This directly contradicts Section 5's earlier statement and introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, creating an in-text contradiction about the confidentiality obligations after termination. The phrase 'unless stated otherwise' adds more confusion
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall come into force on the Effective Date and shall automatically expire and terminate 5 year( s) from the Effective Date. This agreement may be terminated by either Party (i) at any time on fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other Party; and (ii) immediately upon written notice of the other Party’s breach of any term of the Agreement. The Agreement will continue in full force and effect for 2 years after termination. Upon the termination of the Agreement or at any time upon the Disclos ing Party’s written request, Receiving Party will at its expense return all c onfidential information or destroy the same and certify in writing as to such destruction within thirty (30) business days after the date of such destruction. The confidentiality obl igations set out in this Agreement will survive expiry and/or termination of this Agreement for a period of two (2) yea rs, unless stated otherwise. One archival copy of the Confidential Information shall be retai ned by the Receiving Party for internal audit purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the survival period of confidentiality obligations from one year to two years (as per the explanation). The contradicted text contains the changed text. The addition of 'unless stated otherwise' introduces ambiguity about the confidentiality obligations after termination, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document, because it changes a formerly fixed term.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of England and the Parties agree to sub mit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts for any and all disputes, claims and indifference arising out of, or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of Scotland and the Parties agree to sub mit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts for any and all disputes, claims and indifference arising out of, or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement is governed by the laws of Scotland, but disputes will be resolved in English courts. This could lead to confusion and jurisdictional disputes, as Scottish law might be interpreted in an English court, or vice versa. The contradiction occurs within the same sentence, making the conflict apparent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of Scotland and the Parties agree to sub mit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts for any and all disputes, claims and indifference arising out of, or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement is governed by Scottish law but disputes will be resolved in English courts. This creates a direct conflict as Scottish law may be interpreted in an English court, or vice versa, leading to potential confusion and jurisdictional disputes. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, highlights the inherent contradiction within the same sentence, making the conflict clear and impactful. This adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
